_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.base_sha: optional — pinned merge-base the diff was computed against — makes gate verification deterministic; set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit (computed from base_sha)
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Pin the diff base to eliminate the recurring diff_sha256 staleness REWORKs. The binding hash was sha256 of `git diff <base>...HEAD`, a 3-dot diff whose merge-base is re-derived from origin/main at BOTH stamp time and gate time. In a high-merge-velocity repo that merge-base drifts (an agent stamps against a stale local origin/main while the gate fetches a fresher one), producing a different hash and a spurious REWORK on content-clean PRs. This records the exact merge-base commit as a new optional receipt field `base_sha` and computes/verifies diff_sha256 as `git diff <base_sha>..HEAD` (2-dot from the pinned commit — byte-identical to the old 3-dot but deterministic). The gate also asserts base_sha is a real ancestor of the default branch so a forged base cannot hide changes. Fully back-compatible: receipts without base_sha fall back to the legacy origin/main 3-dot path.

validation_plan.reason: Type-checks the new base_sha field, the shape.ts helpers (gitMergeBase/isAncestor/gitDiffExcludingReceiptFrom), and the threaded MechanicalFields.baseSha — all must compile cleanly.

Runs the full suite including the new pin-diff-base.test.ts (pinned verification is deterministic after main advances; old-format receipt verifies via fallback; a non-ancestor base_sha is REJECTED; integrity + protected-path floor still enforced) and the new receipt-write base_sha refresh/staleness tests.
Rebuilds the committed dist/index.js action artifact so it does not drift from src/ (the dist-check CI job enforces this).

result_summary: Added optional `base_sha` to the receipt schema (types.ts); added gitMergeBase/gitDiffExcludingReceiptFrom/gitChangedFilesFrom/isAncestor helpers to shape.ts; shapeCheck now verifies against the pinned base (2-dot) with an ancestry backstop when base_sha is present, else falls back to the legacy 3-dot. `plumb receipt --write`, `stamp`, and `new` all record base_sha and compute the hash from it; refreshMechanical/checkMechanical pin and staleness-check it. Bumped to 0.4.0 (additive + back-compat minor), CHANGELOG + AGENTS.md updated, dist/index.js rebuilt. Verified: tsc clean, 243/243 tests pass including 8 new tests covering deterministic-after-advance, old-format fallback, non-ancestor rejection, content-mismatch integrity, and the protected-path floor. self_modifying:true because src/shape.ts and src/types.ts are protected paths — routed to human review by design.
